title: Display Cubbies Wall Shelf
summary: A wall shelf built entirely from fence pickets, notched and slotted together into a grid of open display cubbies — no screws through the front faces, just interlocking notches, glue, and finish nails.
description:
This shelf's whole structure comes from notched joinery rather than butt joints and screws — every horizontal shelf board gets a set of matching notches cut to interlock with the vertical dividers, so the grid assembles more like a puzzle than a typical screwed-together shelf. **Mark and consistently track which edge you measured each notch from** — fence pickets vary slightly in actual thickness from board to board, and a notch measured from the wrong reference edge on even one board throws off the whole grid's alignment.

Dry-fit the notched shelves into the vertical dividers before committing to glue, since interlocking notch joints can be snug enough to need a mallet or some sanding at tight spots to seat fully. Once everything seats correctly, mark the top and bottom shelf positions to match the notch-outs and attach with finish nails and glue.

The back is genuinely optional and, in the source build, done with more fence picket boards rather than a plywood panel — a cheaper and arguably better-looking choice for a piece built entirely from picket stock. The top gets trimmed to final size with a jigsaw as the last step, after the whole grid is assembled and square.

tools.note: Cutting notches and trimming the top.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut the vertical dividers
steps.body: Cut the vertical dividers to length. Mark and clearly track which side you measure from for all subsequent cuts, since board thickness varies slightly between pickets.

steps.title: Cut the shelf notches
steps.body: Cut notches into five of the seven horizontal shelf boards, marking the same reference side on every board to keep the grid square and even.

steps.title: Assemble the grid
steps.body: With the vertical dividers' marked end down, slide the shelves into place, marked ends consistently to the same side. Use a mallet to seat tight notches or sand as needed.

steps.title: Attach the top and bottom shelves
steps.body: Mark the top and bottom shelf positions to match the notch-outs from step 2, matching the reference side. Nail with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the sides and back
steps.body: Cut and attach the two side boards. Add the optional back boards, cutting each to fit.

steps.title: Trim the top and finish
steps.body: Cut the top down to final size with a jigsaw. Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Wall shelf with notched grid of display cubbies
